options: Can't bind const to A&; use const A&.
OK: const binds to A& in copy ctor.
Copy ctors cannot take references in C++.
Move ctor is selected automatically here.
correct_answer: Can't bind const to A&; use const A&.

question: Which of the following constructors is NOT a copy constructor (i.e., does not count as the copy constructor for special member generation)?
options: X(X) (pass-by-value; not copy ctor).
X(const X&) (copy ctor).
X(X&) (copy ctor).
X(const volatile X&) (copy ctor form).
b=a fails; c(a) works with explicit.
Both work; explicit is ignored here.
Both fail; explicit copy ctors are illegal.
c(a) fails; b=a works.
correct_answer: b=a fails; c(a) works with explicit.

options: User dtor blocks implicit move ops.
User dtor deletes copy ops too (Rule of 3/5).
User dtor makes type trivially movable.
No effect on implicit moves.
correct_answer: User dtor blocks implicit move ops.

options: 0 (C++17 guaranteed elision). under stated conditions
1 (copy from return temporary).
2 (copy return, then copy into x). based on available context
Depends on optimization flags.
Copy deleted; move OK (no custom dtor).
Copy OK; unique_ptr deep-copies by default.
Copy OK; move disabled by unique_ptr.
Neither works: any pointer member blocks moves.
correct_answer: Copy deleted; move OK (no custom dtor).

options: Template ctor isn't copy ctor; implicit copy may exist.
Template ctor is copy ctor; implicit copy not declared.
Any ctor template deletes all special members.
Templates never help copying.
correct_answer: Template ctor isn't copy ctor; implicit copy may exist.

options: Base() deleted; Derived must init Base(other).
OK: Base is copied even if omitted in init list.
Only runtime slicing problem.
Base copy cannot be defaulted.
correct_answer: Base() deleted; Derived must init Base(other).

options: Slicing into vector<Base>; use pointers/clone().
Deep copy occurs because Base has a virtual destructor.
Ill-formed: vector cannot store polymorphic bases.
dynamic_cast is used during copy.

question: During std::vector reallocation, which situation commonly causes the vector to COPY elements instead of MOVE them (when both are available)?
options: Move not noexcept; vector may copy for safety.
Noexcept move exists; vector still copies always.
Movable-only types are copied via memcpy on realloc.
Copy is always preferred.
correct_answer: Move not noexcept; vector may copy for safety.

options: Strong guarantee if swap noexcept; self-assign safe.
operator= must take const T& only in C++ by the standard.
Pass-by-value disables copy and move.
Always shallow-copies.
correct_answer: Strong guarantee if swap noexcept; self-assign safe.

question: Why is a constructor of the form T(T other) almost always a bug when intended as a copy constructor?
options: Pass-by-value isn't copy ctor; needs copy/move first.
Best copy ctor; always faster than const& in practice.
Required for C++17 copy elision to work.
It disables the destructor.
correct_answer: Pass-by-value isn't copy ctor; needs copy/move first.
